_meta.instrument: Open Enneagram of Personality Scales (OEPS v2.0) — 한국어 번역
_meta.full_name: Open Enneagram of Personality Scales, version 2.0 (bipolar / semantic-differential item version) — Open-Source Psychometrics Project (Korean translation)
_meta.license: Creative Commons, variant UNSPECIFIED. The OSPP About page states the site's tests are "made available under a creative commons license on this website" but names no specific variant. The v2.0 ODT artifact contains no embedded CC notice (checked content.xml, meta.xml, manifest.rdf, styles.xml — no license/copyright string), and the OEPS pages carry no per-item CC notice. A BY-NC-SA 4.0 variant is commonly inferred from sibling OSPP pages but is NOT printed on the OEPS materials, so the exact variant is uncertain. Non-commercial research reuse is safe under any CC variant; commercial reuse would require confirming the variant with the author (info@openpsychometrics.org).
_meta.reuse_notes: 한국어 번역(파생물). 비상업 1단계용. LLM 번역이며 검증된 한국어 표준검사가 아님 — 실사용 전 재검수 권장. 라이선스는 원본을 따르되 번역은 파생물이므로 CC-SA/연구용 조건이 그대로 적용됨.
_meta.attribution: Open-Source Psychometrics Project (openpsychometrics.org). The OEPS was developed by the OSPP website (project run by an anonymous author/maintainer) as an open-source Enneagram assessment. Cite as: OSPP Enneagram of Personality Scales v2.0, Open-Source Psychometrics Project, https://openpsychometrics.org/tests/OEPS/ (development docs and ODT at /tests/OEPS/development/). · 한국어 번역: deepinme 번역 워크플로우(2026-07, LLM).
_meta.scoring_notes: Response scales: Q1-Q36 Likert 1-5 (1=Disagree, 3=Neutral, 5=Agree). Q37-Q54 bipolar 1-5 (1=Entirely like left pole, 3=In between, 5=Entirely like right pole). Type score = sum of listed terms; assigned type = highest score. Verbatim scoring formulas from the ODT: Type 1 = Q1 + Q10 + Q19 + Q28 + (6-Q38) + (6-Q41) + Q46; Type 2 = Q2 + Q11 + Q20 + Q29 + (6-Q42) + Q45 + Q47; Type 3 = Q3 + Q12 + Q21 + Q30 + (6-Q40) + Q39 + (6-Q48); Type 4 = Q4 + Q13 + Q22 + Q31 + Q38 + Q44 + Q49; Type 5 = Q5 + Q14 + Q23 + Q32 + Q42 + (6-Q44) + Q50; Type 6 = Q6 + Q15 + Q24 + Q33 + Q37 + Q40 + Q51; Type 7 = Q7 + Q16 + Q25 + Q34 + Q41 + (6-Q43) + (6-Q47) + (6-Q52); Type 8 = Q8 + Q17 + Q26 + Q35 + (6-Q37) + (6-Q45) + (6-Q53); Type 9 = Q9 + Q18 + Q27 + Q36 + (6-Q39) + Q43 + (6-Q54). Type 7 sums 8 items; every other type sums 7. Per-item factor/keyed values above were derived from these formulas and re-verified: contributor counts match exactly. Type names (conventional, NOT printed in ODT which labels only Type 1..9): 1 Reformer/Perfectionist, 2 Helper/Giver, 3 Achiever/Performer, 4 Individualist/Romantic, 5 Investigator/Observer, 6 Loyalist/Loyal Skeptic, 7 Enthusiast/Epicure, 8 Challenger/Protector, 9 Peacemaker/Mediator.
_meta.cautions:
SCOPE: v2.0 has 54 items total. Q1-Q36 are Likert single statements whose wording is IDENTICAL to the already-collected v1.0 36-item set but RENUMBERED and REORDERED (e.g., v2.0 Q10 'I am a perfectionist' = v1.0 Q1; v2.0 Q1 'I strive for efficiency' = v1.0 Q2). The genuinely NEW v2.0 content is Q37-Q54: 18 bipolar semantic-differential items (rate 1-5 between two opposing poles). All 54 are included here so the v2.0 instrument and its full multi-keyed scoring are complete; de-duplicate against v1.0 if you only want net-new items.

ITEM COUNT DISCREPANCY: A WebFetch summary of the /development/ directory prose mentioned '57 items' - that is inaccurate. The actual v2.0 ODT contains exactly 54 items (Q1-Q54), matching the live test page at /tests/OEPS/ (updated 21 January 2026), which states 'The test has 54 statements.' expected_item_count = 54.

MULTI/REVERSE KEYING: Bipolar items are multi-keyed. 10 of the 18 bipolar items (Q37-Q45 and Q47) load on TWO types with opposite scoring direction; the other 8 (Q46, Q48-Q54) load on one type. Reverse-keying is expressed as (6-Q) in the source. Verified: per-type contributor counts derived from the items exactly match the ODT scoring formulas (see scoring_notes).

LICENSE: Creative Commons but variant is NOT printed on any OEPS artifact or page; treat as uncertain. Safe for non-commercial research use; confirm the variant with the author before any commercial reuse or verbatim commercial republication. Consistent with the existing repo's items.oeps.json (v1.0), which flags the same license uncertainty and marks safe_for_commercial_reuse=false.

VALIDITY: The Enneagram is not an empirically validated model of personality (the OSPP page itself states 'The Enneagram is not a model used in psychological research'). Present results as a self-reflection tool, never as diagnosis. Type labels in parentheses (Reformer, Helper, etc.) are conventional Enneagram names added here for clarity; the ODT itself scores only 'Type 1'..'Type 9'.

FORMAT NOTE: Bipolar item 'text' is given as 'leftpole (left pole) - rightpole (right pole)'; the poles are verbatim, while '(left pole)/(right pole)' labels and the em dash are editorial connectors (the source lays the poles in two table columns with a 1-5 rating scale between them). | 번역본: 문항 순서·채점방향(keyed)·factor/facet은 원본과 동일. text_en에 원문 병기.

translation.method: LLM faithful translation (deepinme workflow, 2026-07) — 충실 번역이며 '쉬운말 리라이팅'은 별도 단계
translation.status: working draft — NOT a validated Korean instrument

qa.reviewer_overall: Verified 54/54 items with unique IDs (jq length + unique), one Korean rendering per source item — count matches. Fidelity is high: every item preserves the measured construct and first-person self-report framing, including the impersonal English stems (Q2, Q21, Q30) idiomatically recast as 나는/나에게 statements, and adaptations like 'leave the room'→'그 자리를 떠난다' (Q9) and 'I cry'→'나는 잘 운다' (Q22, natural and true to the trait intent). The 18 bipolar adjective pairs use consistent A↔B formatting with generally precise antonyms. Naturalness is native-level: items read as fluent, idiomatic questionnaire Korean, and the uneven '~는 편이다' softening (Q1/Q5/Q8/Q11/Q24/Q25) is standard self-report register, not an error. The five flagged issues are all polish-level and do not lower the high fidelity rating: Q37 and Q51 are pole-word precision/shading (insecure→self-doubt; fearful→outlook), Q33 is a register mismatch (충직하다), and Q8/Q30 are minor literalness/phrasing. A strong, publishable set with a few optional refinements.

issues.problem: For the confident↔insecure pole, '불안해하는' reads as 'anxious' and half-collides with Q49's '불안정한' (unstable). 'Insecure' on this axis is specifically self-doubt.
issues.suggestion: 자신감 있는 ↔ 자신 없어 하는 (or 자신 없는)

issues.problem: '충직하다' for 'I am loyal' carries a retainer/subordinate register (used of subjects, servants, dogs), which is off for a first-person self-description.
For hopeful↔fearful (an outlook axis), '겁이 많은' shades toward the dispositional trait 'timid/cowardly' rather than fearful/apprehensive about the future.
'리더로 떠오르는' for 'emerge as a leader' is slightly literal/translationese; 떠오르다 is a somewhat mechanical calque of 'emerge' here.
issues.suggestion: 나는 자연스럽게 리더 역할을 맡게 되는 편이다 (or ...리더가 되는 편이다)

issues.problem: '나에게 돈은 행복에 중요하다' leaves the possessor of 행복 implicit and stacks two topic markers (나에게 / 돈은), reading slightly loose; 'my happiness' becomes generic happiness.
issues.suggestion: 돈은 나의 행복에 중요하다 (or 나의 행복에는 돈이 중요하다)

verification.summary: 번역 QA(2차 에이전트): fidelity=high, naturalness=natural, count_match=True, issues=5

items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 1 (The Reformer/Perfectionist)); scored: +Q1 in Type 1

items.text: 나에게 삶에서 가장 중요한 것은 다른 사람들과의 관계다.
items.text_en: My relationships with others are what my life is about.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 2 (The Helper/Giver)); scored: +Q2 in Type 2
positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 3 (The Achiever/Performer)); scored: +Q3 in Type 3

items.text: 나는 사랑에 빠진 모습을 공상하곤 한다.
items.text_en: I daydream about being in love.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 4 (The Individualist/Romantic)); scored: +Q4 in Type 4

items.text: 나는 감정을 드러내는 것을 어려워하는 편이다.
items.text_en: I have a hard time showing emotions.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 5 (The Investigator/Observer)); scored: +Q5 in Type 5

items.text: 나는 이용당할까 봐 두려워서 남을 더 신뢰하지 못한다.
items.text_en: Fear of being taken advantage of keeps me from being more trusting.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 6 (The Loyalist/Loyal Skeptic)); scored: +Q6 in Type 6

items.text: 나는 늘 새로운 경험을 해야만 한다.
items.text_en: I must always be having new experiences.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 7 (The Enthusiast/Epicure)); scored: +Q7 in Type 7

items.text_en: I naturally emerge as a leader.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 8 (The Challenger/Protector)); scored: +Q8 in Type 8

items.text: 나는 다른 사람들이 다투고 있으면 그 자리를 떠난다.
items.text_en: When other people are arguing, I leave the room.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 9 (The Peacemaker/Mediator)); scored: +Q9 in Type 9
positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 1 (The Reformer/Perfectionist)); scored: +Q10 in Type 1
positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 2 (The Helper/Giver)); scored: +Q11 in Type 2

items.text: 나는 남들 눈에 띄는 것을 좋아한다.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 3 (The Achiever/Performer)); scored: +Q12 in Type 3

items.text: 나는 달콤씁쓸한 기분을 느끼는 것을 정말 즐긴다.
items.text_en: I really enjoy feeling bitter sweet.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 4 (The Individualist/Romantic)); scored: +Q13 in Type 4

items.text: 나는 혼자서 취미에 몇 시간씩 몰두한다.
items.text_en: I spend hours alone with my hobbies.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 5 (The Investigator/Observer)); scored: +Q14 in Type 5

items.text: 나는 결정을 내리기 전에 다른 사람들의 의견을 구한다.
items.text_en: I get input from others before I make a decision.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 6 (The Loyalist/Loyal Skeptic)); scored: +Q15 in Type 6

items.text: 나는 누구와도 어떤 주제로든 대화를 계속 이어갈 수 있다.
items.text_en: I can keep a conversation going with anyone about anything.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 7 (The Enthusiast/Epicure)); scored: +Q16 in Type 7

items.text: 나는 아무도 의견이 일치하지 않는 대화를 좋아한다.
items.text_en: I like a conversation where no one agrees.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 8 (The Challenger/Protector)); scored: +Q17 in Type 8

items.text: 나는 문제를 일으키지 않으려고 내 생각을 속에만 담아 둔다.
items.text_en: I keep my thoughts to myself, to prevent trouble.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 9 (The Peacemaker/Mediator)); scored: +Q18 in Type 9

items.text: 나는 다른 사람들이 해 놓은 일을 다시 해야 하는 경우가 많다.
items.text_en: I often have to redo other peoples work.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 1 (The Reformer/Perfectionist)); scored: +Q19 in Type 1

items.text: 나는 다른 사람들이 목표를 이루도록 돕는 데서 큰 만족을 얻는다.
items.text_en: I get lots of satisfaction from helping others achieve their goals.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 2 (The Helper/Giver)); scored: +Q20 in Type 2

items.text: 나는 하루 일정이 빼곡히 계획된 채로 아침을 맞는 것이 좋다.
items.text_en: It is good to wake up to a full day of planned activities.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 3 (The Achiever/Performer)); scored: +Q21 in Type 3
positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 4 (The Individualist/Romantic)); scored: +Q22 in Type 4

items.text: 나는 대부분의 시간을 무언가를 이해하려 애쓰는 데 쓴다.
items.text_en: I spend most of my time trying to understand things.

positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 6 (The Loyalist/Loyal Skeptic)); scored: +Q24 in Type 6
positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 7 (The Enthusiast/Epicure)); scored: +Q25 in Type 7

items.text_en: I want people to tell me the truth, not spare my feelings.

items.text_en: I am very accepting and flexible.

items.text: 나는 내 물건을 잘 정돈해 둔다.
items.text_en: I keep my belongings in order.
items.keyed: positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 1 (The Reformer/Perfectionist)); scored: +Q28 in Type 1
positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 2 (The Helper/Giver)); scored: +Q29 in Type 2

items.text_en: Money is important to my happiness.

items.text: 나는 기성 체제보다 저항하는 쪽의 편을 든다.
items.text_en: I side with the rebels over the establishment.

positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 5 (The Investigator/Observer)); scored: +Q32 in Type 5

positive (Likert 1-5, 1=Disagree 5=Agree; higher = more Type 9 (The Peacemaker/Mediator)); scored: +Q36 in Type 9

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'confident', 5=entirely like right 'insecure', 3=in between). Type 6 (The Loyalist/Loyal Skeptic) positive (+Q37: higher/right-pole 'insecure' raises Type 6); Type 8 (The Challenger/Protector) reverse (6-Q37: lower/left-pole 'confident' raises Type 8)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'rational', 5=entirely like right 'whimsical', 3=in between). Type 1 (The Reformer/Perfectionist) reverse (6-Q38: lower/left-pole 'rational' raises Type 1); Type 4 (The Individualist/Romantic) positive (+Q38: higher/right-pole 'whimsical' raises Type 4)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'unambitious', 5=entirely like right 'driven', 3=in between). Type 3 (The Achiever/Performer) positive (+Q39: higher/right-pole 'driven' raises Type 3); Type 9 (The Peacemaker/Mediator) reverse (6-Q39: lower/left-pole 'unambitious' raises Type 9)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'extrovert', 5=entirely like right 'introvert', 3=in between). Type 3 (The Achiever/Performer) reverse (6-Q40: lower/left-pole 'extrovert' raises Type 3); Type 6 (The Loyalist/Loyal Skeptic) positive (+Q40: higher/right-pole 'introvert' raises Type 6)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'orderly', 5=entirely like right 'chaotic', 3=in between). Type 1 (The Reformer/Perfectionist) reverse (6-Q41: lower/left-pole 'orderly' raises Type 1); Type 7 (The Enthusiast/Epicure) positive (+Q41: higher/right-pole 'chaotic' raises Type 7)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'touchy-feely', 5=entirely like right 'distant', 3=in between). Type 2 (The Helper/Giver) reverse (6-Q42: lower/left-pole 'touchy-feely' raises Type 2); Type 5 (The Investigator/Observer) positive (+Q42: higher/right-pole 'distant' raises Type 5)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'wild', 5=entirely like right 'tame', 3=in between). Type 7 (The Enthusiast/Epicure) reverse (6-Q43: lower/left-pole 'wild' raises Type 7); Type 9 (The Peacemaker/Mediator) positive (+Q43: higher/right-pole 'tame' raises Type 9)

items.text_en: never cries (left pole) — often crying (right pole)
items.keyed: bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'never cries', 5=entirely like right 'often crying', 3=in between). Type 4 (The Individualist/Romantic) positive (+Q44: higher/right-pole 'often crying' raises Type 4); Type 5 (The Investigator/Observer) reverse (6-Q44: lower/left-pole 'never cries' raises Type 5)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'harsh', 5=entirely like right 'gentle', 3=in between). Type 2 (The Helper/Giver) positive (+Q45: higher/right-pole 'gentle' raises Type 2); Type 8 (The Challenger/Protector) reverse (6-Q45: lower/left-pole 'harsh' raises Type 8)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'disorganized', 5=entirely like right 'self-disciplined', 3=in between). Type 1 (The Reformer/Perfectionist) positive (+Q46: higher/right-pole 'self-disciplined' raises Type 1)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'poisonous', 5=entirely like right 'nurturing', 3=in between). Type 2 (The Helper/Giver) positive (+Q47: higher/right-pole 'nurturing' raises Type 2); Type 7 (The Enthusiast/Epicure) reverse (6-Q47: lower/left-pole 'poisonous' raises Type 7)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'social climber', 5=entirely like right 'nonconformist', 3=in between). Type 3 (The Achiever/Performer) reverse (6-Q48: lower/left-pole 'social climber' raises Type 3)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'stable', 5=entirely like right 'unstable', 3=in between). Type 4 (The Individualist/Romantic) positive (+Q49: higher/right-pole 'unstable' raises Type 4)

items.text: 사람을 상대하기 좋아하는 유형 ↔ 사물을 다루기 좋아하는 유형
items.keyed: bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'people-person', 5=entirely like right 'things-person', 3=in between). Type 5 (The Investigator/Observer) positive (+Q50: higher/right-pole 'things-person' raises Type 5)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'spontaneous', 5=entirely like right 'deliberate', 3=in between). Type 7 (The Enthusiast/Epicure) reverse (6-Q52: lower/left-pole 'spontaneous' raises Type 7)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'thick-skinned', 5=entirely like right 'sensitive', 3=in between). Type 8 (The Challenger/Protector) reverse (6-Q53: lower/left-pole 'thick-skinned' raises Type 8)

bipolar semantic-differential (1=entirely like left 'neutral', 5=entirely like right 'opinionated', 3=in between). Type 9 (The Peacemaker/Mediator) reverse (6-Q54: lower/left-pole 'neutral' raises Type 9)
